description: Data-backed route-shape repairs where the generated planner split a human-recognizable field route into artificial segment-ownership buckets.

repairs.block_name: Camel's Back / Kestrel / Crestline / Lower Hulls even-day
repairs.replacement_title: Lower Hulls / Hulls Interpretive / Crestline / Kestral / Red Cliffs after completed frontside cleanup
field_notes: After the 2026-06-20 dashboard sync, Owl's Roost, Gold Finch, 15th Street, and Chickadee Ridge are completed credit and must not remain inside the route 2 source geometry as stale repeat mileage.

Rebuild the current route card from only the remaining Lower Hulls / Hulls Interpretive / Crestline / Kestral / Red Cliffs official segments; completed frontside segments may appear later only if connector audits prove they are unavoidable.

repairs.block_name: Sweet Connie / Shingle / Sheep Camp / Stack Rock
repairs.title: Dry Creek / Sheep Camp / Shingle natural loop

trailhead.name: Dry Creek / Sweet Connie roadside parking

repairs.return_description: Return to the car by continuing down Dry Creek after the Shingle climb.
field_notes: Field route is lower Dry Creek access, Dry Creek lower stem to the Sheep Camp/Dry Creek junction, Sheep Camp out-and-back for segment 1653, Shingle Creek uphill for ascent credit, then Dry Creek down to the car.
This absorbs the stale Sheep Camp-only 16A-2 card because the incremental cost from the same Dry Creek junction is lower than a separate car-to-car outing.
This intentionally repeats lower Dry Creek as lollipop-stem mileage; it must not be represented as a Shingle out-and-back.
Dry Creek segment ownership moves out of 15B so the phone packet no longer splits one field route into two artificial packages.
